scenarios.title: Readable docs stay live and aligned from the repo bundle
scenarios.summary: The repo now keeps the product, marketing, and planning docs in sync through the same governed next-version path that ships the discover package.

truthfulness_summary: The docs slice is largely grounded in persisted workspace artifacts, but the broader control surface still needs stronger release-level truth signaling.

dimension_scores.rationale: The repo-native path now turns promoted product truth into readable docs without a separate PM rewrite loop.
The current docs are readable, traceable, and strong enough to act as the standard aligned package for the reference workspace.
Live-doc sync, validation, and Ralph review all passed on the same bundle now used by the next-version control surface.
The docs now refresh through one governed loop and no longer need an extra manual translation step before internal adoption.
The aligned docs bundle is now part of the repeated next-version baseline rather than a one-off V4 proof artifact.

reviewer_verdict.summary: The readable-doc path remains one of the strongest slices and is good enough for internal default use.

tester_verdict.summary: Live-doc sync and validation remain clean, but broader promotion should wait for the control-surface hardening to finish.

manual_verdict.summary: Keep the readable-doc bundle as the default internal alignment path while the stable slice remains bounded.

feedback_items.summary: Keep the docs slice internal-use until the broader control surface stops overstating promotion status.
feedback_items.recommended_action: Retain docs as the default internal alignment path and rescore after the remaining truthfulness fixes land.

next_action: Use the readable-doc bundle internally, but do not let it hide broader truthfulness gaps elsewhere in the control surface.
